[91:23]First Lady of the United States.

[91:32]Jared Kushner. You see Jared Kushner in the back?

[92:07]Members of Congress and my fellow Americans,

[92:12]our nation is back, bigger, better, richer,

[92:15]and stronger than ever before.

[92:19]Yeah?

[92:23]Is it?

[92:29]Less than five months from now our country will celebrate an epic milestone in American history

[92:36]the 250th anniversary of our glorious

[92:41]American independence

[92:43]This July 4th, we will mark two-and-a-half centuries of liberty and triumph, progress

[92:49]and freedom in the most incredible and exceptional nation ever to exist on the face of the Earth.

[92:55]And you've seen nothing yet.

[92:57]We're going to do better and better and better.

[92:59]This is the golden age of America.

[93:05]I don't know about that one, Chief.

[93:16]When I last spoke in this chamber 12 months ago, I had just inherited a nation in crisis

[93:23]with a stagnant economy, inflation at record levels, a wide open border.

[93:30]horrendous recruitment for military and police,

[93:34]rampant crime at home, and wars and chaos all over the world.

[93:38]But tonight, after just one year, I can say with dignity

[93:43]and pride that we have achieved a transformation

[93:46]like no one has ever seen before,

[93:49]and a turnaround for the ages.

[93:51]It is indeed a turnaround for the ages.

[94:20]And we will never go back to where we were just a very short time ago.

[94:26]Today our border is secure.

[94:28]USA!

[94:50]The roaring economy is roaring like never before,

[94:54]and our enemies are scared, our military and police are stacked,

[94:59]and America is respected again, perhaps like never before.

[115:07]From 1776 to today, every generation of Americans has stepped forward to defend life, liberty,

[115:17]And they're really doing it for the next generation.

[115:21]But now it's our turn.

[115:22]Together, we're building a nation where every child

[115:25]has the chance to reach higher and go further,

[115:29]where government answers to the people, not the powerful,

[115:32]and where the interests of hardworking American citizens

[115:35]are always our first and ultimate concern.

[115:39]That is the debt we owe to the heroes who came before us,

[115:43]and that is the promise we must keep to America

[115:46]for our 250th year.

[115:53]by passing the largest tax cuts in American history

[115:57]and our Republican majorities delivered so beautifully.

[190:37]He is one more heroic American

[190:42]Navy fighter pilot, Royce Williams served in World War II, Korea, Vietnam, flying more

[190:49]than 220 missions.

[190:52]In disguise over Korea in 1952, Royce was in the dogfight of a lifetime, legendary dogfight.

[191:01]Flying through blizzard conditions, his squadron was ambushed by seven Soviet fighter planes

[191:07]It was his first aerial combat of the war.

[191:10]And despite being massively outnumbered and outgunned,

[191:13]Royce led the takedown of four enemy jets

[191:15]and almost destroyed the others,

[191:19]vanquishing his adversaries while taking 263 bullets

[191:24]to his own plane and being seriously hurt.

[191:28]His story was secret for over 50 years.

[191:30]He didn't even want to tell his wife.

[191:32]But the legend grew and grew.

[191:35]But tonight, at 100 years old, this brave Navy captain

[191:40]is finally getting the recognition he deserves.

[191:44]He was a legend long before this evening.

[191:47]Royce, please stand up.

[191:49]And I will ask the First Lady of the United States

[191:52]to present Captain Royce Williams

[191:54]with his Congressional Medal of Honor.

[194:29]Thank you both very much, Eric. Thank you.

[194:52]250 years is a long time in the life of a nation.

[194:56]But in other sense, it's really a mere moment

[194:59]in the eye of history.

[195:02]Two of the gentlemen we met in the gallery this evening

[195:04]took their first breaths one century ago,

[195:08]100 years before that, on July 4th, 1826,

[195:14]the author of the Declaration of Independence.

[195:18]Brilliant.

[195:22]Just a single long human lifespan separates the giants who declared and won our independence

[195:29]from the heroes who stand among us tonight.

[195:32]Everything our nation has done, everything we have achieved, has been the work of those

[195:37]few great lifetimes.

[195:39]In those brief chapters, Americans built this nation from 13 humble colonies into the pinnacle

[195:46]of human civilization and human freedom, the strongest, wealthiest, most powerful, most

[195:52]successful nation in all of history.

[195:55]Americans ventured out across the daunting and dangerous continent we carve past through

[196:05]settled a boundless frontier and tamed the beautiful,

[196:08]but very, very dangerous, Wild West.

[196:13]From empty marshes and wide-open plains,

[196:16]we raised up the world's greatest cities.

[196:20]Together, we mastered the world's mightiest industries

[196:23]and shattered histories, monstrous tyrannies.

[196:27]And we liberated millions from the chains

[196:29]of fascism, communism, oppression, and terror.

[196:33]Americans lifted humanity into the skies on the wings of aluminum and steel and

[196:39]then we launched mankind into the stars on rockets powered by sheer American

[196:44]will and unyielding American pride. We wired the globe with our ingenuity.

[196:52]We captivated the planet with American culture and now we are pioneering the next great American breakthroughs that will change the entire world.

[197:03]All of this and so much more is the enduring legacy and unmatched glory of the hard-working patriots who built and defended this country and who still carry the hopes and freedoms on all of humanity's backs.

[197:20]For years, they were forgotten, betrayed, and cast aside.

[197:24]But that great betrayal is over, and they will never be forgotten again.

[197:29]Because when the world needs courage, daring, vision, and inspiration,

[197:33]it is still turning to America.

[197:36]And when God needs a nation to work His miracles,

[197:41]He knows exactly who to ask.

[197:44]There is no challenge Americans cannot overcome.

[197:48]No frontier too vast for us to conquer.

[197:51]No dream too bold for us to chase.

[197:55]No horizon too distant for us to claim.

[197:58]For our destiny is written by the hand of Providence,

[198:03]and these first 250 years were just the beginning

[198:08]from the rugged border towns of Texas

[198:10]to the heartland villages of Michigan.

[198:13]From the sun-kissed shores of Florida to the endless fields of the Dakotas.

[198:18]And from the historic streets of Philadelphia

[198:21]to right here in our nation's capital, Washington, D.C.,

[198:26]the golden age of America is upon us.

[198:30]The revolution that began in 1776 has not ended.

[198:35]It still continues because the flame of liberty

[198:38]and independence still burns in the heart

[198:41]of every American patriot.

[198:43]And our future will be bigger, better, brighter, bolder,

[198:48]and more glorious than ever before.

[198:51]Thank you.

[198:52]God bless you.

[198:53]And God bless America.

[198:56]OK.
